description_long: This is a medium-sized, fossilized ammonite stone with a distinct spiral shell pattern visible on its broadest face. The stone displays a mix of dark gray, black, and white coloration, with a marbled, mottled appearance and some areas of higher contrast. One side features a visible fossil spiral, while the opposite side is more solid and less detailed, with a rougher, uneven surface. The edges are irregular and jagged, suggesting natural breakage or erosion, and a small label with handwritten text is affixed to one side, indicating it may be part of a collection or study. The surface is hard and rough, with a matte finish and little to no reflectivity. This stone would likely be found as a decorative or educational feature in an urban park, garden, or museum setting.
description: A fossilized ammonite stone with a spiral shell pattern, marbled gray and black coloration, and rough, jagged edges. It has a hard, matte surface and a small label attached.
description_view_0: The broad, flat face shows the spiral fossil pattern and mottled gray-black coloration, with a visible label on the lower right.
description_view_1: A narrow edge view reveals the stone's thickness, jagged profile, and the label partially visible on the side.
description_view_2: The opposite broad face is less detailed, mostly dark with some marbling, and the spiral is less pronounced.
description_view_3: Another edge view, showing a mostly white and gray surface with a rough, uneven texture and no visible label.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on a plinth or ground
attribute_pattern: Distinct spiral fossil pattern with irregular marbling and mottling across the surface.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and matte, with signs of natural erosion and some minor chipping along the edges.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured urban landscaping or display cases, drawing attention as a natural artifact.
